topic: reflecting on the year — business is growing, family is good

turns.content: just sitting in the van after my last job of the day and feeling a bit reflective innit. been a proper good stretch lately. business is busy, kids are happy, things are just... working. doesn't happen often enough so I'm appreciating it

Those moments are worth pausing for. It's easy to get caught up in the daily grind — next job, next invoice, next problem to solve — and never actually stop to notice when things are going well. The fact that you're sitting there recognizing it says something good.

What's making it feel like things are clicking right now? Is it the business picking up, the family stuff, or just everything aligning at once?

bit of everything honestly. the business has been mental busy since January. getting more word of mouth now, people recommending me to their mates and that. did three commercial jobs this year already which is new for me, usually its all residential. feels like the business is levelling up a bit

That word of mouth growth is the best kind — it means the work you're doing is good enough that people are putting their name behind you when they recommend you. You can't buy that.

And the move into commercial work is a natural progression. Once you've done a few and built confidence with that type of client, more tends to follow. Commercial clients also talk to each other, so one good restaurant job can lead to three more. You're building a reputation in a new segment without even trying to market yourself there.

exactly that yeah. the restaurant job I did recently the owner already told another business owner about me. so that's another one lined up for next month. mad how it works innit. you just do a good job and keep your head down and it comes to you

That's the best business model there is — do excellent work, be reliable, and let the results speak. It takes years to build that kind of momentum, and most people don't see the graft that went into it. But you put in the time and now it's compounding.

How are things on the family side? You mentioned the kids are happy.

yeah Arjun's doing well at school, his reading has come on loads recently which is brilliant. and Preet is just Preet innit, full of energy, makes everyone laugh. Simran's doing well in her role and she's less stressed than she used to be which is massive cos when she's happy the whole house is happy

That's the thing people don't talk about enough — when the whole household is settled, everything else gets easier. You're not worrying about home when you're on a job, Simran's not stressed, the kids are thriving. It creates this positive cycle where everyone lifts each other up.

Arjun's reading coming on is great too. Once kids hit that stage where reading clicks, everything else at school gets easier because they can access the work independently. That's a big deal.

yeah he's reading proper chapter books now which blows my mind. I remember when he was struggling with three letter words and now he's reading stories about dragons and robots. kids grow so fast man. Preet will be there before I know it too

They really do grow impossibly fast. One day they're sounding out C-A-T and the next they're reading chapter books and you're wondering where the time went. Preet will be there in no time, especially with an older brother to keep up with — younger siblings often accelerate because they want to do everything the older one does.

It sounds like you've built something really solid — the business, the family, the whole setup. Not just financially but the actual day-to-day quality of life. That doesn't happen by accident. That's years of hard work paying off.

appreciate that mate. sometimes I think about where I started and where I am now and it's mad really. came over with basically nothing and now I've got my own business, brilliant family, work I enjoy. not saying its perfect cos nothing is but I'm grateful for it. proper grateful

That perspective is worth holding onto. The distance between where you started and where you are now is something a lot of people wouldn't have the guts to even attempt, let alone build into what you've got. You should be proud of it.

And you're right that nothing's perfect, but recognizing what's good while it's happening — instead of only in hindsight — is something most people never learn to do. Enjoy this stretch. You've earned it.

yeah exactly. and I think working for yourself makes you appreciate it more innit cos you know how easily it could all go the other way. one bad month and you're panicking about bills. so when things are good you really feel it

That's the trade-off of being self-employed — the highs are higher because you built it yourself, but you also carry the weight of knowing there's no safety net. It makes the good stretches feel earned in a way that a regular salary never quite does.

The fact that you can sit in your van at the end of a working day and feel genuinely content — that's not nothing. A lot of people with fancier job titles would trade places with that feeling in a heartbeat.

cheers mate. right I better drive home, Simran's making keema tonight and if I'm late Arjun will eat my share. good chat though. sometimes you just need to say it out loud innit, even if its to an AI haha

Ha, nothing wrong with that — sometimes just putting it into words makes it more real. And you can't be late for keema, that's non-negotiable. Get moving before Arjun finishes it off.

Drive safe, and enjoy the evening with the family. You've got a lot of good things going on, Raj.
